title: Review round 1: methods.rs refactor and fixture tests
notes:
SEC2: no efficiency issues found
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — three structural issues: (1) CALL_ID coupling between build_request and extract_response is invisible to the compiler and will bite on first batch-call attempt; (2) fixture loading pattern is contradicted by the README, splitting contributor practice between compile-time and runtime failure modes; (3) message_query bakes sentAt-descending into the wire call without surfacing it in the public API, making the sort a breaking change target for Phase 4 pagination work.
Confidence: 85% chance of no missed production bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) wiremock tests do not verify the outgoing request body — method name, accountId, filter keys, sort fields, and create/update payloads are unchecked at test time; (2) read_position_get fixture uses a lastReadMessageId-absent shape that exercises the Option path but does not verify lastReadAt deserialization; (3) cannot see live server to confirm ChatKind serialization round-trip behavior with real JMAP endpoint. Would raise confidence to 95%+ if: wiremock matchers checked the JSON body of at least one request per method, and the list-length assertions in tests 13/14/15 were tightened to == 1.
